title: Psychotria megistosticta
intro: Psychotria megistosticta, also known as the Brazilian bartera, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its potential to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria megistosticta include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Psychotria megistosticta is often prepared as a decoction or infusing its leaves in hot water to make a tea, and it is also used to make tinctures and infusions for various health benefits.
